mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling period, or housing starts fail to correlate with equity returns when retail sales are rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed cases during recovery phases, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or the monthly correlation between CPI changes and 5-day forward returns remains above -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP levels exceeding 13000 confirm robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 surpasses 13000, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate validate labor market tightening and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when payrolls rise alongside unemployment, or interaction terms show zero marginal predictive value in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indices indicate contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines consistently precede positive 5-day SPX returns during tightening phases, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 45% in out-of-sample testing.
